zotero.abstractNote: Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) are powerful generative models capable of producing high-quality images. However, they are typically black-box models: given a trained generator, it is difficult to find a latent code that produces an image with specific desired features. In this paper, we propose a method to make GANs content-addressable. Given a target image or a set of images, we want to find a latent code that generates an image similar to the target. We formulate this as a kernel mean matching problem in the latent space. We show that our method is effective for finding latent codes that produce images with desired attributes, and for performing image-to-image translation tasks.
